_meta.description: Machine-readable map of the docs tree, routing-critical entry surfaces, and update ownership.
notes: AGENTS.md is the canonical prose repo contract.
Compatibility surfaces should stay thin and point back to AGENTS.md and docs/catalog.json.

repo_state.posture: v0b Starter Loop is the M001 base. See docs/status/current-state.md for live posture, dated history, and active conditions.

environment_contract.notes: Editing from Windows Cursor is fine; repo automation assumes WSL/bash.

repo_state.stack: Vite + React + TypeScript + PWA + Dexie (schema v5)
repo_state.blocking_gate: D130 founder-use conditions tracked in docs/research/founder-use-ledger.md; D91 retention gate deferred. See docs/status/current-state.md for current reads.

use_when: routing to the right doc family
checking which doc family owns a concept
deciding whether a finding belongs in canon or research
writing a new design or UX review pass
looking for prior design reviews and their recommendations
tool expects a Claude-specific root file
tool expects a lightweight root summary
tool expects a small JSON manifest

docs.canonical_for: product principles and strategic stance
decision status
scope, workflow, object model, MVP requirements
phase sequencing and exit criteria
current project posture, recent shipped history, and active validation gates
discovery surface for design and UX guidelines and review history; pointer-only (does not move existing canonical design docs)

point-in-time UX baseline of the v0b Starter Loop after the 2026-04-22 partner-walkthrough polish and the 2026-04-23 walkthrough-closeout polish; classifies findings as editorial-class, founder-session-trigger-gated Tier 1b, D91-field, or decision-blocked; does not unlock Tier 1b and does not modify D86, D91, D119, D125, D129, D130, or D132

Per-drill verdict table for the 15 inactive `m001Candidate:false` reserve records in `app/src/data/drills.ts`. Records source / dependency / reachability / reference state, keeps all records parked behind `graduate-when`, `hold-pending-D101`, or `hold-pending-M002` verdicts, and applies no retire removals.
evidence base and interpretive framing for the D91 M001 go/no-go retention gate
minimum scored-contact threshold, self-scoring bias guardrails, and product policy shape for the binary Good/Not Good progression gate
evidence base for first-run screen count, standalone vs folded safety-gate placement, and progressive profiling (sharpens O11; seeds O16)
cross-jurisdiction regulatory posture (US/EU/UK/Canada/Australia) for the pain-gate + deterministic load rule + stop/seek-help bundle; avoid-phrases list and copy-audit findings supporting D86
evidence base and ship-ready defaults for the mandatory warm-up and cool-down blocks in short (10–25 min) beach sessions (sharpens D85; seeds D105)
evidence base for the coach-facing extension shape and the paid coach premium model question (flips O1 lean; seeds D106, D107, D108; adds centralized-marketplace to ruled-out)

evidence base for the 'opinionated default + bounded escape hatches' posture, the six structured-feedback patterns, and AI-as-explainer wiring (reinforces D6/D11/D20/D21/D37/D43-D46/D74/D98; sharpens V0B-11; seeds O17)

post-ACWR literature synthesis for the deterministic sRPE-load engine: baseline3 + peak30 + rolling-14-day variables, precedence-ordered rule table, minimum-history phases, and the +5-10% / +10-15% / +20% bands tuned for amateur 1-3 sessions/week skill-dominant use (sharpens D84; seeds D113)

operational posture on O13 (persistent team identity for beach pairs); forward-compatible participant/profile/consent schema; graduation gate before a first-class Team object ships (resolves O13; seeds D114, D115, D116, D117)

operational protocol for the M001 14-day validation cohort (recruitment / consent scripts, per-use micro-log and session-ledger schema with confidence flag and separate researcher-interpretation column, three signal-based pulses at days 3/7/11, 72-hour non-returner probe, preregistered one-page decision memo, role separation and readout-ordering rules, and the 5-page evidence packet); operationalizes D91's banded reading without changing thresholds

curated mineable takeaways from the VDM (Volleyball Canada Development Matrix) primary source: 4-stage skill vocabulary (Initiation / Acquisition / Consolidation / Refinement), time-to-consolidate quote (~3 months at 2-3×/week), 3×/week development-minimum rule, Structural Tolerance framing, 0-10 pain scale with cumulative threshold, Adaptation Principle citation, and cue-reading/decision-making/execution demand taxonomy

product-useful extractions from the indoor-biased FIVB Level I (2013) and Level II (2016) coaches manuals: Fitts and Posner three-stage motor-learning model, one-cue-at-a-time rule for beginners, 2-3-trials-between-feedback cadence, whole-practice bias and its four exceptions, federation-level drill measurement-mode vocabulary, four drill design types, Yakovlev supercompensation windows, injury-distribution context, and the ruled-out indoor/team-tactics list; indexes the secondary PDFs in docs/research/sources/reference-only/

decision-relevant distillation of external vendor evidence on self-administered baseline skill assessments (passing / serving / setting) for adult recreational beach volleyball players (2-5 years, 1-3 sessions/week, self-coached); 3 of 3 vendors reconciled (all received 2026-04-22); asymmetric recommendation: serve baseline on Costa 2024 graduated 2-22 concentric rings + Zetou 2005 zone overlay + optional vendor-2 5/5 side-switch (optional Ðolo 2023 float-specialist alternate for standing-float servers); partner-mode primary for pass and set with three compatible source lineages (Zetou 2005 beach-validated, VSAT AAHPERD-derived, NCSU Bartlett 1991 r=0.73 passing / r=0.88 overhand-pass/setting); solo-no-wall pass and set are control drills not baselines (3-of-3 vendor agreement on framing, vendor 3 sharpest); 10 scored trials + 1-2 familiarization per accuracy test (30s x 2-3 trials for wall tests); outcome-vs-physical-marker rubrics only, never self-rated technique quality; Lidor 2007 fatigue overlay dropped on vendor-3 direct empirical contradiction (null rested-vs-exertion result); environmental posture is standardize collection + within-player comparison, do not cross-normalize scores; self-scoring r ~= 0.47 athletics anchor independently confirmed by Mabe & West 1982 (vendor 1) and Karpen review (vendor 3); biphasic bias (vendor 2 mechanism) and outlier-anchoring / best-moment bias (vendor 3 via Guenther 2015) flagged as candidate open questions for binary-scoring-progression.md arguing against monotonic D104 correction without re-parameterizing; 3-of-3 unanimity that no volleyball-specific self-scoring evidence exists; pre-registered Synthesis-stability bars with 3-vendor evaluation trace recorded in-place; informs any future Phase 1.5 baseline-test surface; does not modify D80, D104, D121, or O12

decision-relevant distillation of external vendor evidence on cross-skill (pass / serve / set) Pearson r for adult recreational beach players (2-5 years, self-coached); 3-of-N vendors reconciled (vendor 1 central r ~= 0.45 range 0.30-0.65; vendor 2 central r ~= 0.32 range 0.25-0.40; vendor 3 central r ~= 0.50 range 0.35-0.65); three-vendor unanimity on per-skill vector over single scalar; reconciled working estimate r ~= 0.35-0.50 (median 0.45, mean 0.42) with plausible envelope 0.25-0.65 (low-to-moderate confidence); vendor 3 contributes direct developmental-rate asymmetry data (Gabbett 2006 +76/+335/+40/+15% over 8 weeks), within-rally coupling data (Palao 2019 N=5,161 receptions), the within-rally-vs-person-level distinction, the cross-sectional-vs-change-score distinction, and a hybrid architectural refinement (per-skill vector + general-factor onboarding prior + partial spillover + UI-only scalar projection) compatible with V1/V2; vendor 3 triggered pre-registered Bar 2 (inside-envelope recenter + new evidence types) without crossing Bar 1 (scalar-defensibility); Synthesis-stability section now records the vendor-3 evaluation trace in-place; validates per-skill posture of drills / chains / progression gates and sets the design default for any future user-visible 'skill status' rollup; does not modify D121 (vendor 3 structurally validates current D121 framing), D80, or D104; usable standalone at 3-of-N with explicit fold-in instructions for vendor N+1

decision-relevant distillation of external vendor evidence on safely introducing the jump-float serve to adult amateur beach volleyball players in a coach-less context (feeds d36 Jump Float Introduction authoring when Tier 1b fires + pre-scaling safety-review track O7); 3 of 3 vendors reconciled (all received 2026-04-22); vendors 1 and 2 converged at ~30-45 reps/session; vendor 3 diverged sharply at 3x4=12 reps first-exposure anchored on three independent mechanisms (Hurd 2009 IHP early-phase 4-6 serves/set template; 87-serve study showing clinically meaningful infraspinatus fatigue that athletes did not reliably perceive; 2025 prospective cohort prior-injury OR 25.3 / scapular-dyskinesis OR 4.2); synthesis resolves to vendor 3's first-exposure spec under the written-down conservative-wins-on-safety principle (docs/research/2026-04-22-research-sweep-meta-synthesis.md); three-vendor convergence on bow-and-arrow + in-front-contact + rigid-hand cue triplet, 48h between sessions, categorical do-not-ship subpopulations (prior dislocation, symptomatic SLAP / RC tendinopathy, Beighton 5/9 + instability, age 50 without overhead-throwing history); vendor 3 adds mandatory volleyball-specific warm-up before unlock (VolleyVeilig RCT -21% acute injury), slide jump-float explicit intermediate step, Hurd soreness rules as warning-sign copy, no-jump-topspin-in-same-session rule; vendor 2 adds monthly infraspinatus photo check (Lajtai 2009 30% prevalence typically painless), ACWR monitoring, annual 4-6 weeks off-overhead, PROMs (KJOC / FAST) with licensing flagged; 8 enumerated do-not-ship-without-physio-input blockers; single biggest gap is no peer-reviewed dose-response in amateur self-coached beach (all three vendors agree; product opportunity); reinforces D82, D83, D85, D88, D105 without re-parameterizing them; seeds docs/plans/2026-04-22-tier1b-serving-setting-expansion.md d36 courtsideInstructions volume default at 3x4=12 first-exposure when the Tier 1b gate fires (via pointer not activation); does not open a physio-review work item for O7 today; does not unblock Tier 1b or elevate d36 to Tier 1a

cross-topic readout of the 2026-04-22 three-wave research day (skill correlation + per-skill baseline-tests + jump-float); names the conservative-wins-on-safety principle the jump-float wave forced explicit, with scope (safety-bearing ship specs only; mechanism citation required on conservative anchor) and falsification conditions; names the research-velocity substitution failure mode (research-heavy periods with zero founder-use ledger advance are Condition 1 / Condition 2 warning signs per the adversarial memo); applies D1 (this catalog + docs/research/README.md pointer entries), D2 (founder-use-ledger red-team reminder), D3 (docs/roadmap.md Phase 1.5 baseline-tests spec-input pointer), D4 (docs/roadmap.md Risks-and-mitigations research-substitution + safety-spec-vendor-outlier rows); D5 (O21 partial narrowing), D6 (binary-scoring-progression biphasic + outlier-anchoring flag), D7 (Tier 1b d36 authoring pointer) are flagged needs-review and are not applied by the note alone; ranked next-steps table aligned to current goal (advance founder-use ledger -> 2026-05-21 Condition 3 final close -> 2026-07-20 D130 re-eval); explicit restraint list (no Tier 1b unblock, no Phase 1.5 activation, no new O# IDs, no re-parameterization of D80 / D104 / D121)

decision input for the post-2026-04-26 pair rep-capture question (Tier 1b P2-3 trigger fired by the founder pair pass session). Lays out four framings (A per-drill difficulty tag at Transition; B per-drill Good/Total at Transition; C in-session running tally on RunScreen; D hybrid tag-required + counts-optional) and scores each against six decision criteria (D104 50-contact math preservation, RunScreen attention cost, post-session typing pain, authoring-budget consumption, pair/solo voice asymmetry per D125 + D132, honesty test). Names the cross-cutting facts the framing decision must not forget (today's UI already deviates from V0B-12 drill-variant-grain spec, the 2026-04-23 QuickTagChips deletion is grain-different from the proposed per-drill tag, the full successMetric.type enum needs a coherent capture story, D131 telemetry stays off so a Dexie migration is in scope, and contract-changing framings need a D### proposed-decision row before the spec patch). Does not pick a framing and does not propose label vocabulary; the recommendation lives in docs/decisions.md (new D### row) and the spec patch lands in docs/specs/m001-review-micro-spec.md.

Validated findings from the 2026-04-27 founder + Seb pair pass session against the cca2 build (post-`D133` per-drill capture, post-Tier-1b Layer A drill authoring, post-2026-04-27 solo-vs-pair sweep). Eight findings (F1-F8): (F1) Difficulty chip surface gates to `main_skill | pressure` block-types, so a 25-min pair pass session with one main_skill swap got exactly one chip — by-spec but under-instrumented; the 'only on new drills' framing was coincidence (the swap landed on a Tier 1b-authored drill). (F2) Pass-counter coverage is two distinct gaps: gap 2a — non-count main-skill drills (streak / points-to-target / pass-grade-avg / composite) capture Difficulty only; gap 2b — count-eligible drills at non-main_skill slots (today's `d10-pair` 6-Leg Monster at technique, `d03-pair` Continuous Pass at movement_proxy) capture nothing at all. (F3) Second confirmation of the Tier 1b in-session running rep counter trigger (1 of ≥2 hits toward the post-`D133` Framing C re-trigger). (F4) Tier 1c focus-picker + skill-level-mutability second hit — Seb's voice memo asks ('30 minutes of serving', 'change my level day-to-day', 'choose what skills to drill') are partner-walkthrough-class evidence; skill-level mutability recorded as separate line item, not bundled into Tier 1c. (F5) `d26` cooldown copy hard-coded ~3 min while workload allows 3-6 min; today's 4-min wrap left a ~1.5 min 'what do I do?' gap. (F6) `d03` Continuous Passing fits movement_proxy slot only nominally; today's session inverted `d10-pair` (movement-tagged) into technique while stationary `d03-pair` landed at movement_proxy. Two later red-team findings (F7 solo movement_proxy content gap, F8 skill marker absent from per-block eyebrow plus setup-led courtside copy) added the same day. Same-day fixes shipped for F5, F6, and the F1 follow-up role/skill eyebrows; F1/F2 routed to docs/plans/2026-04-27-per-drill-capture-coverage.md. Provenance: founder chat dump + Seb voice memo + Dexie export volley-drills-export-2026-04-27.json.

Qualitative field feedback from the 2026-04-28 founder + Seb build-17 no-net pair dogfeed. Positives: drill subcategories / role-skill cues were useful, and optional count capture felt right because counting was not forced. Frictions: cooldown/sub-block pacing beeps were missing or inaudible, and the landing/entry surface still did not make changing difficulty or skill level obvious. Strengthens existing audio-pacing reliability and skill-level-mutability evidence tracks; adds an optionality constraint to any future Framing C live-counter design. Does not fire a new implementation plan by itself.

Follow-up investigation for the build-17 F3 cooldown/sub-block beep report. Confirms the Safety Continue audio/wake-lock path, the Run preroll wake-lock release regression, active warmup/wrap subBlockIntervalSeconds coverage, and which cooldown audio cues exist. Records the narrow app-side fix and preserves the platform boundary: silent switch, manual lock, and unsupported/denied Wake Lock remain residual.

Durable architecture principles, layer rules, extension points, testing posture, review gates, anti-patterns, and a feature-routing checklist for the app/ workspace. Written for future agents adding features or refactors. Routes to canonical product docs (`docs/vision.md`, `docs/decisions.md`, `docs/prd-foundation.md`), `app/README.md` for current state, and `.cursor/rules/*.mdc` for focused rules — does NOT duplicate them. Update only when a layer boundary, extension-point pattern, review gate, or testing pyramid materially changes.

Tier 1b Layer A drill-authoring plan: shipped four source-backed drill records (`d31`, `d33`, `d40`, `d42`) plus safe same-chain progression links for `chain-6-serving` and `chain-7-setting`, without schema/filter/UI/Dexie changes. Defers `d36 Jump Float Introduction` to `O7` sports-medicine / PT review and `d43 Triangle Setting` to `D101` 3+ player support; current Layer A cap consumption is 4/10.

Tier 1b implementation plan for the post-2026-04-26 pair rep-capture work unlocked by the P2-3 founder-session trigger (`docs/research/founder-use-ledger.md` 2026-04-26 row). Implements `D133` (Framing D from `docs/research/2026-04-26-pair-rep-capture-options.md`): new `/run/check` route after completed blocks with required per-drill **Difficulty tag** plus optional per-drill **Good/Total**, Transition refocused on Up Next, Review-screen session-level count card removed for count drills, Complete drill-grain aggregation, plus a forward-only Dexie migration (`perDrillCaptures: PerDrillCapture[]`). Authors zero new drill records (does not consume the drill-record cap; sibling `docs/plans/2026-04-22-tier1b-serving-setting-expansion.md` consumes 4/10). Defers Framing C (in-session counter on RunScreen) under explicit re-trigger condition. Spec contract in `docs/specs/m001-review-micro-spec.md` §'Per-drill capture at Transition (D133)'.

Fourth successive editorial-class polish pass (after 2026-04-22 / 2026-04-23 / 2026-04-26) targeting the genuinely-open Tier 1b items in `docs/research/partner-walkthrough-results/2026-04-22-all-passes-reconciled.md`. Two items shipped: `R11` lifted the Home Skip-review confirm from an inline two-step row into a centered `role=dialog` `SkipReviewModal` matching `RunScreen`'s `End session early?` shape (safe-primary `Never mind` first, danger `Yes, skip` below; `Esc` closes; aria-modal + aria-labelledby + autofocus + Tab trap + restore-focus-on-close); `R13` added a quiet `Logged: N session{s} · H:MM total` line above the Settings privacy promise, gated on `count > 0`, sourced from a new `getSessionTallySummary()` query that mirrors `formatDurationLine` per-session minute math and excludes `discarded_resume` stubs via `isTerminalSession`. Two items demoted on close inspection: `R7` (Beach Prep Three truncate-with-expand — underlying blob no longer holds, `d28` reformatted 2026-04-21, and the 2026-04-22 Transition parity pass deliberately removed truncate-with-expand) and `R14` (Accent color demotion — audit-class scope, deferred to a separate pass). Authors zero new drill records; no Dexie migration; no metadata schema changes.

Implementation routing for the two per-drill capture coverage gaps surfaced by the 2026-04-27 cca2 dogfeed (`docs/research/2026-04-27-cca2-dogfeed-findings.md` F1 + F2). **Phase 1 (gap 2b) shipped 2026-04-28** as part of the architecture pass U2 capture-domain consolidation (`11fed34`): the eligibility decision lifted into `app/src/domain/capture/eligibility.ts::resolveDrillCheckCaptureEligibility`, keyed off the metric-type strategy registry (`metricCapturesCounts`) so count-eligible passing drills at technique / movement_proxy slots (e.g., `d10-pair` 6-Leg Monster, `d03-pair` Continuous Pass) now trigger `eligible_counts` regardless of slot type — only `warmup` / `wrap` are excluded. Resolver returns three states: `eligible_counts`, `eligible_difficulty_only`, `bypass`. Coverage proven by `app/src/domain/__tests__/drillCheckCapture.test.ts` (8 cases) plus `DrillCheckScreen.perDrillCapture.test.tsx` for screen-tier wiring. Phase 2 (gap 2a) authors per-`successMetric.type` capture stories so streak / points-to-target / pass-grade-avg / composite drills gain a rep-shaped capture surface beyond the Difficulty chip — **still gated** on a separate trigger (≥2 founder-ledger sessions where Difficulty-only is explicitly noted as insufficient, OR partner walkthrough ≥P1, OR Phase 1 has been live for ≥4 sessions). Authors zero new drill records (Phase 1 consumed one Tier 1b authoring-attention slot; Phase 2 budget held). Spec contract amended in docs/specs/m001-review-micro-spec.md §'Per-drill required field' + §'Non-count drills at main_skill / pressure (gap 2a)'.

Catalog-authoring sweep that closes the partner-voice gap on the active M001 drill set. Authors 8 new tuned Pair variants on `m001Candidate: true` Drill families (`d01`, `d05`, `d22`, `d31`, `d38`, `d39`, `d40`, plus a `d33-solo-net` rewrite-and-split into `d33-solo` + `d33-pair`); tightens `participants.max = 1` on the now-redundant Solo voices (`d22-solo`, `d33-solo`, `d38-solo`, `d39-solo`); renames `label: 'Solo'` to `label: 'Any'` on the broad-participation warmup/cooldown variants (`d25-solo`, `d26-solo`, `d28-solo`) so their existing `participants.max=14` doesn't trip the new rule; equipment.balls tightened from `'many'` to `1` on `d31-pair` and `d33-pair` so the pair-rounds reality (one ball, partner cadence) is honest and so `hasUnmodeledRequirements` doesn't filter them out of swap pools. Adds a permanent regression guard via a new `participants_label_mismatch` rule in `app/src/data/catalogValidation.ts`. Lifts the pair-mode menu from 'leaky solo voice' to a first-class pair drill catalog matching `D132`'s pair-first product stance. M001 catalog footprint stays at 19 active Drill records (variant count grows; Drill count unchanged); no schema bump, no Dexie migration, no new archetype rules.

Post-D133 Layer 2 queue closeout pass. Ships two reliability fixes first (`DrillCheckScreen` waits for per-drill capture persistence before navigating; draft edit Setup hydration reads `getCurrentDraft().context` instead of the last completed context), adds Playwright 375 px /review hit-area coverage against current controls, and demotes only decorative accent/readout uses (`PerDrillCapture` Quick tag eyebrow, `PassMetricInput` % good readout, Safety heat-tip bullets). Records `Beach Prep Three` truncate-with-expand as killed, and auto-fill training recency plus Net/Wall persistence as deferred behavior changes requiring a dedicated setup/safety/defaults spec.

Ten-unit architecture systematization pass for the app/ workspace. Lands the in-flight refactor as atomic commits (U0); fixes the field-merging review-draft write so Drill Check captures and Review form fields cannot clobber each other (U1); consolidates capture eligibility / merge / aggregate / meaningful-draft / window classification into one closed `app/src/domain/capture/` module with a `MetricTypeStrategy` registry (U2); decomposes `services/review.ts` along its true reasons-to-change into `services/review/{submit,expire,drafts,cohort,bundle}` behind a barrel (U3); introduces `app/src/model/` as the pure product-type home and demotes `app/src/db/types.ts` to a thin re-export adapter so `domain/` no longer depends on Dexie row shapes (U4); pulls browser-runtime concerns into `app/src/platform/` and hoists post-block routing into `app/src/domain/runFlow/postBlockRoute.ts` (U5); lays forward-compatibility seams `SessionParticipant[]` (`D115`/`D116`/`D117`), `SkillVector` (`D121`), `ExportSession` adapter (Phase 1.5), and `CoachPayload` type (Phase 2 / `D106`) without shipping new UX (U6); encodes the `docs/vision.md` P12 contract structurally in `app/src/contracts/screenContracts.ts` and reframes `runFlowInteractionContract` as a transitional characterization spec with a written sunset rule (U7); refreshes app-facing docs and the testing pyramid map in `app/README.md` and `.cursor/rules/` (U8); consolidates durable building guidance in `docs/ops/app-architecture-guidance.md` (U9); closes with a holistic red-team gate (U10). No Dexie schema bump, no new state-management framework, no UI changes during the architecture pass itself.

Spec-gap fix that re-aligned the per-drill capture surface (`DrillCheckScreen` + `PerDrillCapture`) with `docs/specs/m001-review-micro-spec.md` §Required (V0B-28 forced-criterion prompt, D104 layer-1 self-scoring bias correction). **Shipped 2026-04-28** as part of the architecture pass U2 capture-domain consolidation (`11fed34`). Five items landed: (1) `getBlockSuccessRule` helper in `app/src/domain/drillMetadata.ts` mirrors `getBlockMetricType`'s variant-resolution rule, and the success rule renders as `Success rule: <rule>. If unsure, don't count it as Good.` inside the expanded `Add counts` body inside `PerDrillCapture` so the chip-only fast path stays calm (pinned by `app/src/components/__tests__/PerDrillCapture.test.tsx` `describe('V0B-28 forced-criterion prompt', ...)` and `app/src/domain/__tests__/drillMetadata.test.ts` `describe('getBlockSuccessRule', ...)`); (2) `PassMetricInput`'s rate label changed from the pass-specific `% good pass rate` to the skill-neutral `% good`; (3) `d33 Around the World Serving`'s `successMetric.description` re-worded from a session-level zone enumeration to the per-attempt rule `Serve lands in the called zone.` on both Solo and Pair siblings (`target` retained as the session goal); (4) wire-up — eligibility resolver in `app/src/domain/capture/eligibility.ts` returns `successRule` on both eligible branches, `useDrillCheckController` exposes `captureSuccessRule`, `DrillCheckScreen` passes through; (5) spec sync — `primarySkillMetric` block now reads `the drill check surface (post-D133)` with the source-from-record clause and a 'Surface-move history (2026-04-27)' addendum. Legacy `ReviewScreen` fallback path intentionally out of scope (reached only by non-D133 legacy sessions). Authors zero new drill records; no Dexie migration; no metadata schema changes. Verification: 1065/1065 Vitest, lint clean, build clean.

Post-Tier-1b-Layer-A next-chunk ideation, refreshed 2026-04-29 after D135. Current sequence: catalog reserve audit first, then Tier 1c focus picker, then skill-level override, then founder/Seb evidence loop. The 2026-04-29 focus-picker deepening selects the draft-surface decision as the first focus-picker brainstorm target. Preserves the pre-D135 survivor list as historical context and records rejection lines for substitution-class work.

Historical combined requirements doc for pre-D135 Tier 1c prepay plus catalog reserve audit. Refreshed 2026-04-29 with a focused #1 catalog reserve audit brief: verdict table for the 15 m001Candidate:false records, retire-only application, catalog registration, and drills.ts pointer.

Requirements for the D135-fired Tier 1c session focus picker. Resolves the draft-screen ambiguity by selecting a Tune today review step as the v1 surface and codifies the architectural rigor (shared focus resolver, in-transaction regeneration use case, stale-write guards, repeat / recovery focus strip, accessibility semantics, privacy boundaries). PARTIALLY SUPERSEDED 2026-04-30 by docs/brainstorms/2026-04-30-pre-run-simplification-requirements.md: R4/R5 (four-chip Recommended-as-peer), R7/R32 (mandatory routing on every pre-run path), R8 (Safety read-only focus echo), R12 (all chips always enabled), R34 four-value source-aware Back. The architectural decisions and remaining R-IDs stay authoritative when Tune today is invoked under the new opt-in posture.

Post-D135 implementation plan for the triggered bundle: Stream 3 catalog reserve audit (implementation-ready as authored). Stream 1 (Tier 1c focus picker) is owned by docs/plans/2026-04-29-001-feat-tune-today-focus-picker-plan.md; the U1-U5 sketches in this plan are historical and not implementation-ready. Stream 2 skill-level mutability is paused pending Tune today dogfood. Authors zero new drill records and preserves D131 no-telemetry posture.

SUPERSEDED 2026-04-30 by docs/plans/2026-04-30-001-feat-pre-run-simplification-plan.md. Originally specced a mandatory pre-safety Tune today route on every pre-run path, optional SetupContext.sessionFocus, a shared focus resolver consumed by initial picks / build-time substitution / swap alternatives, an in-transaction draft regeneration use case, source-aware Back, repeat- and recovery-flow focus stripping, and a calm shibui screen. The architectural pieces (effectiveFocus.ts, services/session/regenerateDraftFocus.ts, stale-write guards, focus strip on buildDraftFromCompletedBlocks and buildRecoveryDraft) are preserved by reference in the 2026-04-30 plan; routing, chip set, and Safety echo are simplified.

Ranked ideation answering 'how and when do we integrate attacking, team tactics, out-of-system, and live/adaptive layers?'. Seven survivors: forward-only `attack` SkillFocus reservation + `scenario` axis (S1), two-axis skill × scenario catalog reframe (S2), surfacing existing `MetricType` game-like patterns (S3), team-tactics deferred to M003+ (S4), attack clustered with serve as overhead-striking continuum (S5), out-of-system as scenario not skill (S6), live/adaptive as runner-surfacing not catalog expansion (S7). Recommended sequence: S1+S2 schema/docs reservation now (zero cap), trigger-gated attack exposure under D135-style discipline, team tactics deferred to M003+. S1+S2 consumed by docs/brainstorms/2026-04-29-skill-scope-reservation-requirements.md.

Ground-up audit plus synthesis of recent focus/skill ideation answering whether Tune today has enough active, source-backed drill depth for pass/serve/set practices and swaps across solo/pair, equipment, and long-session contexts. Finds Passing broadly covered, Setting buildable but thin, and Serving fragile (pair/no-net likely unbuildable; long serve sessions still pass-flavored in support slots; skill level not modeled). Recommends a coverage grid, serving-focus credibility pass, focus-aligned support-block definition, thin-focus honesty contract, long-session simulator, source-backed backlog cards, and variant-level provenance/adaptation checklist.

SUPERSEDED 2026-04-30 by docs/brainstorms/2026-04-30-pre-run-simplification-requirements.md (Track 4: docs-only) on the grounds that types reservation + research note + catalog cross-links spend attention before D135-style trigger evidence has fired. The replacement records the policy ('out-of-system is scenario, not skill; attack is a future skill axis member when trigger fires; no schema changes until then') in a single decision row instead of a types PR. The skill-correlation insight and the cluster-pairing thesis remain valid as a future-state design check; only the implementation cost is deferred.

Pre-run choice-load simplification across four parallel tracks. Iteration log header (2026-04-30, post-agent-red-team) records: Track 2 reverted from opt-in to MANDATORY Tune today per user feedback (opt-in failed adversarial review on the D135 use case it was built for — 11 taps for first focus session); Track 1 strengthened to FULL Wind delete (field + UI + fixtures + windFriendly catalog cleanup), not 'dead read'; Track 4 demoted from D136 decision row to JSDoc on SkillFocus type union; custom-drill builder captured as post-M001 future exploration. Body below describes the original simplification thesis; the 2026-04-30-001 plan owns what actually ships.

Requirements for making Tune today focus choices fully catalog-backed across pass/serve/set, skill levels, setup configurations, long sessions, and swaps. Establishes that Volleycraft should not have thin or unavailable visible focus states; the catalog must meet a practical floor (2 main options, 1 support option, 1 pressure option where applicable, and 1 same-focus swap per focus-controlled slot) before the focus picker is considered complete. Expansion is variant-first, but catalog readiness overrides the earlier D130 authoring cap when required for focus trust.

Canonical implementation plan (revised 2026-04-30 after agent red-team and user feedback). Ships Tune today MANDATORY between Setup and Safety with four chips (Recommended/Passing/Serving/Setting), all-enabled with fail-on-tap inline copy, source-aware Back ('setup' | 'home'), and the full 2026-04-29-001 architecture: effectiveFocus.ts shared resolver, services/session/regenerateDraftFocus.ts in-transaction use case, stale-write guard, repeat / recovery focus strip. Bundled with three orthogonal pre-run cuts: (1) full Wind delete (field, UI, fixtures, windFriendly catalog cleanup; no Dexie migration); (2) conditional Wall row (only when Solo + No Net) plus Wall-required catalog invariant test; (3) no default Safety focus echo, recovery-override line only when painFlag === true && draft.context.sessionFocus !== undefined. Track 4 ships skill-scope policy as JSDoc on SkillFocus (no D136 decision row). Includes explicit K-ID inheritance matrix from 2026-04-29-001 (K1-K13 with K7 collapsed to two values). Supersedes docs/plans/2026-04-29-001-feat-tune-today-focus-picker-plan.md (its design rationale is inherited; ~6 net-new files ship under this plan).

Sub-ideation under S1 of `docs/ideation/2026-04-28-what-to-add-next-ideation.md`. Ranks variant shapes for the per-move visible pacing indicator on RunScreen (visual treatment / data source / overflow reconciliation / scope / audio coupling). 4 survivors; V1 (highlighted current row + structured pacingSegments + per-move boundary beep + bonus-overflow footnote) selected as shibui-and-minimal.

Requirements for the V1 per-move pacing indicator with the user-directed re-framing to model warmup/cooldown drills as composed `DrillSegment[]`. Covers actors A1–A3, flows F1–F8, acceptance examples AE1–AE6 (including AE6 — visible state independent of audio state, the load-bearing cue-stack invariant). Origin doc for the `2026-04-28-per-move-pacing-indicator` plan.

Implementation plan and shipped ledger for the per-move pacing indicator. Adds `DrillSegment` type + `DrillVariant.segments?` + `courtsideInstructionsBonus?`; authors segments on `d28-solo` (4×45s), `d26-solo` (3×60s), `d25-solo` (5 segments / 60+30+30+30+30; durationMinMinutes bumped 2→3); pure helper at `app/src/domain/runFlow/segmentTiming.ts`; `<SegmentList>` at `app/src/components/run/SegmentList.tsx`; controller wiring in `useRunController` + extension of `useBlockPacingTicks` for per-segment end beep; catalog validation rule `segment_duration_mismatch`. Visible-channel half of S1 from `docs/ideation/2026-04-28-what-to-add-next-ideation.md`.

Phase 2A of `docs/plans/2026-04-27-per-drill-capture-coverage.md`. Ships an optional `Add longest streak (optional)` drawer on `/run/check` for `streak`-typed `main_skill` / `pressure` drills (`d01-solo`, `d01-pair`, `d38-pair`, `d41-pair`). Architecture seam: `captureShape` discriminator added to the metric-strategy registry (semantic only, no UI copy); additive Dexie v6 `metricCapture?: { kind: 'streak'; longest: number }` field on `PerDrillCapture`; new pure-domain builders (`buildPerDrillCaptureRecord`, `validateStreakLongest`) that forbid impossible rows by construction; eligibility resolver extends `eligible_difficulty_only` with `optionalCaptureShape: CaptureShape`. UI: streak drawer is collapsed by default, `inputMode=numeric` whole numbers `0..99`, V0B-28 success rule renders above the input without the anti-generosity nudge (does not apply to streak). CompleteScreen renders `Longest streak: N` quietly under the drill name and suppresses the `Counts not logged` line for streak-logged drills. Pulled forward as a bounded D130 founder-use exception authorized by the new `D134` row, with a pre-registered falsification gate that freezes Phase 2B (`points-to-target`, `pass-grade-avg`) if streak capture is skipped or feels like bookkeeping in the next eligible sessions. `D131` posture preserved: telemetry stays off, export omits `metricCapture` in Phase 2A. Authors zero new drill records; consumes one Tier 1b authoring-attention slot; defers `points-to-target`, `pass-grade-avg`, `composite`, `completion` to Phase 2B.

update_routing.trigger: scope, workflow, or object model changed
major principle or strategic stance changed
phase sequencing or exit criteria changed
agent-facing doc or routing guidance changed
new design / UX review pass written

research_routing.focus: broad product and training research
outdoor UI defaults
experimental calm, restrained, Japanese-inspired visual direction for future UI work; hierarchy, spacing, pacing, and anti-cliche guardrails
rationale for the product name Volleycraft, the 13-test naming rubric, rejected-alternative reasoning, and rename-scope guardrails (supports D125)

iPhone/PWA platform constraints, the 2026 three-layer storage-durability model (ITP 7-day timer / Home Screen carveout / quota-pressure eviction + heuristic persistent mode), iOS 17.4-style IndexedDB regression risk, three-state save copy, and the real-device test protocol (sharpens D57; seeds D118 and O18)
timer and interruption behavior
trust invariants and verification strategy
per-layer depth of investment, fake-indexeddb trust boundary, and deliberate under-investment list for the pre-field test stack
Dexie schema and migration guidance
prototype feedback, retest evidence, and pre-field-test priorities
non-developer field-look feedback on the v0b Starter Loop build; stable VB-FL-NN IDs for findings between feature-complete and D91 cohort kickoff
Phase 1.5 periodization vocabulary stub (PoST framework) for O2 multi-week planning
operational definition of 'solo' environment; default solo archetype and wall-access posture (resolves O4)
minimum scored-contact thresholds, self-scoring bias guardrails, and comparable-product analogues for the binary progression gate (sharpens D80 via D104; keeps O12 open)
evidence base and interpretive framing for the D91 M001 go/no-go retention gate (kill-floor vs go-bar; novelty-effect enrichment signals)
public evidence on first-run screen count, standalone vs folded safety-gate placement, and progressive profiling (sharpens O11; seeds O16)
cross-jurisdiction regulatory posture for the pain-gate + deterministic load rule + stop/seek-help bundle; avoid-phrases list and copy-audit findings supporting D86
evidence base and ship-ready defaults for the mandatory warm-up and cool-down blocks in short (10–25 min) beach sessions (Beach Prep Two/Three/Five, Downshift framing); sharpens D85, seeds D105

opinionated default + bounded escape hatches vs locked plans vs empty scaffolding; six structured-feedback patterns; AI-as-explainer with deterministic reason trace (reinforces D6/D11/D20/D21/D37/D43-D46/D74/D98; sharpens V0B-11; seeds O17)

comparative coach-facing business models (coach-first SaaS, BYOC-lite, centralized expert, hybrid marketplace, institution); flips O1 lean to BYOC-lite; seeds D106, D107, D108; reshapes Phase 1.5 coach clipboard scope and Phase 2 posture

post-ACWR literature synthesis for the deterministic sRPE-load engine: baseline3 + peak30 + rolling-14-day variables, precedence-ordered rule table, minimum-history phases, and the +5-10% / +10-15% / +20% bands for amateur 1-3 sessions/week skill-dominant use (sharpens D84; seeds D113)

persistent team identity posture for beach pairs (stay session-first with forward-compatible participant/consent hooks; no durable Team object now); comparative pair-sport consumer-app patterns; HCI and fitness-adherence evidence; local-first Automerge/Keyhive consent model; resolves O13 operationally and seeds D114, D115, D116, D117

operational protocol for the M001 14-day validation cohort (recruitment / consent scripts, per-use micro-log and session-ledger schema, three signal-based pulses at days 3/7/11, 72-hour non-returner probe, preregistered one-page decision memo, role separation and readout-ordering rules, 5-page evidence packet); operationalizes D91's banded reading without changing thresholds

curated takeaways from the Volleyball Canada Development Matrix PDF with per-item disposition (Adopt / Candidate / Defer): 4-stage skill vocabulary, ~3-months-to-consolidate quote, 3×/week development minimum, Structural Tolerance framing, 0-10 pain scale with cumulative threshold, Adaptation Principle citation, cue-reading/decision-making/execution demand taxonomy

product-useful extractions from the indoor-biased FIVB Level I (2013) and Level II (2016) coaches manuals: Fitts and Posner three-stage motor-learning model, one-cue-at-a-time rule for beginners, 2-3-trials-between-feedback cadence, whole-practice bias and its four exceptions, federation-level drill measurement-mode vocabulary, four drill design types, Yakovlev supercompensation windows, injury-distribution context, and the ruled-out indoor/team-tactics list; indexes docs/research/sources/reference-only/

LTD 3.0 framework overview, Active-for-Life target-user stage mapping with Train-to-Train content scope, Active-for-Life life phases (30s / 40-50s / 50-60s / 70+), Active-for-Life sleep row (do not train if fatigued or sleep deprived), practice-to-compete ratios from the four LTD stage one-pagers, and the Person Pillar Guidebook as a coaching-copy template (desired state / indicators / reinforcing comments / reflection questions); complementary to docs/research/vdm-development-matrix-takeaways.md which owns the narrow VDM items

vendor-evidence distillation on cross-skill (pass / serve / set) Pearson r for adult recreational beach players (2-5 years, 1-3 sessions/week, self-coached); 3-of-N vendors reconciled (vendor 1 central r ~= 0.45 range 0.30-0.65; vendor 2 central r ~= 0.32 range 0.25-0.40; vendor 3 central r ~= 0.50 range 0.35-0.65); three-vendor unanimity on per-skill vector over single scalar; reconciled working estimate r ~= 0.35-0.50 (median 0.45) with plausible envelope 0.25-0.65; vendor 3 contributes direct developmental-rate asymmetry (Gabbett 2006), within-rally coupling (Palao 2019), within-rally-vs-person-level and cross-sectional-vs-change-score distinctions, and a hybrid architectural refinement (general-factor onboarding prior + partial spillover + UI-only scalar projection); Synthesis-stability section now records the vendor-3 evaluation trace (Bar 2 triggered, Bar 1 not crossed); validates per-skill posture of drills / chains / progression gates; sets design default for any future user-visible 'skill status' rollup; does not modify D121, D80, or D104; usable standalone at 3-of-N

vendor-evidence distillation on self-administered baseline skill assessments (passing / serving / setting) for adult recreational beach volleyball players (2-5 years, 1-3 sessions/week, self-coached); 3 of 3 vendors reconciled (all received 2026-04-22); asymmetric recommendation: ship serve baseline on Costa 2024 graduated 2-22 rings + Zetou 2005 zone overlay + optional vendor-2 5/5 side-switch (optional Ðolo 2023 float-specialist alternate); partner-mode primary for pass and set with three compatible source lineages (Zetou 2005 beach-validated, VSAT AAHPERD-derived, NCSU Bartlett 1991 r=0.73 passing / r=0.88 overhand-pass/setting); solo-no-wall pass and set are control drills not baselines (3-of-3 vendor agreement on framing, vendor 3 sharpest); 10 scored trials + 1-2 familiarization per accuracy test; outcome-vs-physical-marker rubrics only, never self-rated technique quality; Lidor 2007 fatigue overlay dropped on vendor-3 null-result contradiction; standardize collection + within-player comparison, do not cross-normalize scores; r ~= 0.47 athletics self-scoring anchor independently confirmed (Mabe & West 1982 by vendor 1; Karpen review by vendor 3); biphasic (vendor 2) and outlier-anchoring / best-moment (vendor 3 / Guenther 2015) flagged as candidate open questions against monotonic D104 correction; 3-of-3 unanimity that no volleyball-specific self-scoring evidence exists; pre-registered Synthesis-stability bars with 3-vendor evaluation trace recorded in-place; informs any future Phase 1.5 baseline-test surface; does not modify D80, D104, D121, or O12

vendor-evidence distillation on safely introducing the jump-float serve to adult amateur beach volleyball players in a coach-less context (feeds d36 Jump Float Introduction authoring when Tier 1b fires + pre-scaling safety-review track O7); 3 of 3 vendors reconciled (all received 2026-04-22); vendors 1 and 2 converged at ~30-45 reps/session; vendor 3 diverged sharply at 3x4=12 reps first-exposure anchored on Hurd 2009 IHP early-phase 4-6 serves/set + 87-serve study showing imperceptible infraspinatus fatigue + 2025 prospective cohort prior-injury OR 25.3; synthesis resolves to vendor 3's first-exposure spec under the written-down conservative-wins-on-safety principle; three-vendor convergence on bow-and-arrow + in-front-contact + rigid-hand cue triplet, 48h between sessions, categorical do-not-ship subpopulations; vendor 3 adds mandatory volleyball-specific warm-up before unlock (VolleyVeilig RCT -21% acute injury), slide jump-float explicit intermediate step, Hurd soreness rules, no-jump-topspin-in-same-session rule; vendor 2 adds monthly infraspinatus photo check (Lajtai 2009), ACWR monitoring, annual off-overhead, PROMs (KJOC / FAST) licensing-flagged; 8 enumerated do-not-ship-without-physio-input blockers; single biggest gap is no peer-reviewed dose-response in amateur self-coached beach; reinforces D82, D83, D85, D88, D105 without re-parameterizing them; seeds d36 courtsideInstructions default at 3x4=12 first-exposure when Tier 1b gate fires (via pointer not activation); does not open a physio-review work item for O7 today; does not unblock Tier 1b or elevate d36 to Tier 1a

cross-topic readout of the 2026-04-22 three-wave research day (skill correlation + per-skill baseline-tests + jump-float); names the conservative-wins-on-safety principle the jump-float wave forced explicit (scope: safety-bearing ship specs only; mechanism citation required on conservative anchor; falsification conditions listed); names the research-velocity substitution failure mode (research-heavy periods with zero founder-use ledger advance are Condition 1 / Condition 2 warning signs per the adversarial memo); applies D1-D4 apply-now deltas (this catalog + research README pointer entries, founder-use-ledger red-team reminder, roadmap Phase 1.5 baseline-tests spec-input pointer, roadmap Risks-and-mitigations research-substitution and safety-spec-vendor-outlier rows); D5-D7 needs-review deltas documented but not applied (O21 partial narrowing, binary-scoring-progression biphasic + outlier-anchoring flag, Tier 1b d36 authoring pointer); ranked next-steps table aligned to current goal (advance founder-use ledger -> 2026-05-21 Condition 3 final close -> 2026-07-20 D130 re-eval); explicit restraint list (no Tier 1b unblock, no Phase 1.5 activation, no new O# IDs, no re-parameterization of D80 / D104 / D121)

qualitative field feedback from the 2026-04-28 build-17 no-net pair dogfeed: drill subcategories / role-skill cues useful, optional count capture appreciated because counting is not forced, cooldown/sub-block beeps missing or inaudible, and the landing/difficulty-setting ask repeated. Strengthens audio-pacing reliability and skill-level-mutability evidence tracks; adds optionality constraint to future Framing C live-counter design; does not fire a new implementation plan by itself

follow-up investigation for build-17 missing cooldown/sub-block beeps: Safety Continue audio/wake-lock path, Run preroll wake-lock release regression, active warmup/wrap subBlockIntervalSeconds coverage, cooldown cue existence, and platform-boundary residuals (silent switch, manual lock, unsupported/denied Wake Lock)
